Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at Model 1s workflow I notice some structural issues

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the decision and interview stages happen concurrently without a defined order meaning hiring decisions could occur before or alongside interviews

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the interview step is not actually required before moving to onboarding which seems like a significant deviation from typical hiring processes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The screening step only connects to the initial posting with no downstream dependencies so it could theoretically happen at any point in the process even after decisions or onboarding

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The onboarding loop allows for redundant repetitions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: most critically the payroll step can be entirely skipped through the XOR gate meaning an employee could be hired and onboarded without ever being added to payroll

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Thats a major violation of normal business operations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Comparing the two Model 1s issues are ordering violations where all required activities still occur once while Model 2 allows mandatory activities to be skipped entirely and has activities decoupled from their logical dependencies

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 is clearly closer to how hiring should actually work
